heading: Expediting approval of modifications and alterations of projects by non-Federal interests
label: 408a Expediting approval of modifications and alterations of projects by non-Federal interests
notes: “Secretary” DefinedSecretary means the Secretary of the Army, see , set out as a note under .section 2 of Pub. L. 113–121section 2201 of this title

children.heading: Section 14 application defined
children.label: a Section 14 application defined
children.text: In this section, the term “section 14 application” means an application submitted by an applicant to the Secretary requesting permission for the temporary occupation or use of a public work, or the alteration or permanent occupation or use of a public work, under (commonly known as the “Rivers and Harbors Appropriation Act of 1899”) ().section 14 of the Act of March 3, 189933 U.S.C. 408

children.heading: Review
children.label: b Review
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Secretary, after providing notice and an opportunity for comment, shall establish a process for the review of section 14 applications in a timely and consistent manner.June 10, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: establish benchmark goals for determining the amount of time it should take the Secretary to determine whether a section 14 application is complete;

children.label: B
children.text: establish benchmark goals for determining the amount of time it should take the Secretary to approve or disapprove a section 14 application; and

children.label: C
children.text: to the extent practicable, use such benchmark goals to make a decision on section 14 applications in a timely and consistent manner.

children.heading: Benchmark goals for determining whether section 14 applications are complete
children.label: A Benchmark goals for determining whether section 14 applications are complete
i
children.text: the Secretary reach a decision on whether a section 14 application is complete not later than 15 days after the date of receipt of the application; and

children.label: ii
children.text: if the Secretary determines that a section 14 application is not complete, the Secretary promptly notify the applicant of the specific information that is missing or the analysis that is needed to complete the application.

children.heading: Benchmark goals for reviewing completed applications
children.label: B Benchmark goals for reviewing completed applications
i
children.text: the Secretary generally approve or disapprove a completed section 14 application not later than 45 days after the date of receipt of the completed application; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in a case in which the Secretary determines that additional time is needed to review a completed section 14 application due to the type, size, cost, complexity, or impacts of the actions proposed in the application, the Secretary generally approve or disapprove the application not later than 180 days after the date of receipt of the completed application.

children.label: A
children.text: provide written notification to the applicant; and

children.label: B
children.text: include in the written notice a best estimate of the Secretary as to the amount of time required for completion of the review.

children.heading: Failure to achieve benchmark goals
children.label: d Failure to achieve benchmark goals

children.text: why the Secretary failed to make a decision in accordance with such process;
the additional actions required before the Secretary will issue a decision; and
the amount of time the Secretary will require to issue a decision.

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: 1 Submission to Congress
children.text: The Secretary shall provide a copy of any written notice provided under subsection (d) to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: all section 14 applications received by the Secretary; and

children.label: B
children.text: the current status of such applications.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 120 days after , the Secretary shall issue guidance on the implementation of this section.December 16, 2016

children.heading: Incorporation
children.label: 2 Incorporation
children.text: In issuing guidance under paragraph (1), or any other regulation, guidance, or engineering circular related to activities covered under , the Secretary shall incorporate the requirements under this section.section 408 of this title

children.heading: Prioritization
children.label: g Prioritization
children.text: The Secretary shall prioritize and complete the activities required of the Secretary under this section.
